# commitment-discipline

Commitment discipline focuses on converting vague verbal commitments into verifiable, trigger-based actions to prevent execution gaps; the core pattern is that untracked promises to Alex expire unless they enter a task queue with observable conditions (when artifact X appears, do Y) rather than time-based delays. Rather than analyzing paralysis, the protocol is to pick a candidate, attach a falsifier, and delegate—the warning signal itself is sufficient. The latest evolution treats commitments as conditional AND-gated triggers that only activate when multiple observable conditions align simultaneously, replacing hollow "defer until next cycle" patterns with concrete constraint-based action.
